summary: Architect analysis complete. Fix #17 reuses existing SoatConfirmationPage by redirecting from vehicle_form_page._formListener after creation when soatLocalPath is set; no cubit/state/contract changes. Fix #21 refactors BlocBuilder in registration_form_content.dart to drive UI from ResultState (initial/loading/data/empty/error) instead of reading availableVehicles directly, with 3 new widget files extracted per coding standards. No backend, no DB, no design, no ARB changes.

changeMap.reason: After successful vehicle creation, if state.soatLocalPath != null push SoatConfirmationPage with savedVehicle + XFile to persist SOAT via existing flow.
Use state.when(...) snapshot instead of reading availableVehicles directly; render loading spinner when initial/loading.
Spinner shown while VehicleCubit is initial/loading.
Extracted empty state with create CTA.

issue17.rationale: SoatModel requires startDate/expiryDate/insurer non-null and the user does not provide these during vehicle creation; SoatConfirmationPage already handles capture + upload + backend persist + VehicleCubit.updateSoatLocally. Reusing it avoids backend changes and avoids placeholder data.
issue17.fallback: If pushReplacement+go_router misbehaves, pop with savedVehicle then push SoatConfirmationPage in Future.microtask.
issue17.errorHandling: If SOAT upsert fails inside SoatConfirmationPage, vehicle remains created; user gets SnackBar — satisfies AC-4.

issue21.rationale: Current builder ignores state snapshot and reads availableVehicles from cubit, which is empty during Loading/Initial. Driving UI from ResultState.when guarantees correct rendering per state.
issue21.fetchTrigger: Out of scope — VehicleCubit fetch remains owned by current callers.

regressionTestSurface: Vehicle creation without SOAT (manual E2E + AC-3)
Cancel SOAT confirmation post-creation (manual E2E + AC-4)
Edit vehicle with existing SOAT (manual E2E)
Registration selector — VehicleCubit Loading state (widget test + E2E + AC-6)
Registration selector — VehicleCubit Data with vehicles (E2E + AC-5)
